Every season, every matchup, every ridiculous team name — the complete record book.

▸Playoff Bracket

Actual recorded playoff-week matchups, round by round. Final placements are in the standings table above.

Every team's weekly total for the selected season, so you can see the whole league's shape at once.

Rivalry Detail

Pick two managers — or click a cell in the matrix above — to see the full history between them.

▸Top 10 Highest-Scoring Team Performances

The best single-week team totals in league history, any season, any manager. Click a row to jump to that box score.

▸Top 10 Highest-Scoring Player Weeks

The best single-week performances by an individual player, league-wide (starters and bench; team defenses excluded). Click a row to jump to that box score.

▸Longest Win Streaks

Best all-time consecutive-win run per manager, counted across season boundaries and through the playoffs. A loss or tie breaks a streak; bye weeks are skipped.

▸League Scoring by Season

Average points per team per week, all games included. Shows how the scoring environment has shifted since 2016.

▸Trade & Waiver Activity

Career trades by manager. Track length reflects trade count relative to the league leader.

▸Activity by Season
▸Bottom 10 Lowest-Scoring Team Performances

The worst single-week team totals in league history, any season, any manager. Click a row to jump to that box score.

▸Biggest Blowouts & Closest Finishes

All-time top 10 largest and smallest margins of victory, across every recorded matchup. Click a row to jump to that box score.

Biggest Blowouts
Closest Finishes
▸Longest Losing Streaks

Worst all-time consecutive-loss run per manager, counted across season boundaries and through the playoffs. A win or tie breaks a streak; bye weeks are skipped.

▸Best & Worst Single-Season Point Totals

Total regular-season points scored, ranked across every manager-season in league history.

Highest-Scoring Seasons
Lowest-Scoring Seasons
▸Lineup Efficiency (Career)

Actual points scored vs. the best possible lineup each week, built from that week's full roster (starters + bench; IR excluded). Higher is better set-and-forget management. Managers with fewer than 5 recorded weeks are left out.

▸Points Left on the Bench (Career)

Total points missed by not starting the optimal lineup, career-wide.

▸Luck Index (Career)

Actual win rate minus "all-play" win rate — the record you'd have if you played every team, every week, instead of your actual schedule. Positive means your record has run hotter than your scoring; negative means the schedule has been tougher than your scoring deserved.

▸Position Power Rankings (Career)

Highest average points per game at each starting position, minimum 10 starts.

▸Consistency (Career)

Standard deviation of weekly score, career-wide — lower means steadier week to week, higher means boom-or-bust. Minimum 10 recorded weeks.

▸Most Moves, Single Season

Waiver-wire activity in one season.

▸Most Trades, Single Season

Trade activity in one season.

▸Biggest Busts

Single-week scores that fell furthest below that manager's own season average.

▸Heartbreak Award — Close Losses (Career)

Most career losses by a single-digit margin (under 10 points). Based on every recorded head-to-head matchup.

▸Nail-Biter Award — Close Wins (Career)

Most career wins by a single-digit margin (under 10 points). Based on every recorded head-to-head matchup.

▸Bench Merchant

Weeks where a manager's bench outscored their starting lineup, career total. A high count is a sign the bench had the better week — and the lineup call didn't.

▸Toilet Bowl, By Year

The last-place finisher each season.

Search every recorded roster spot across all seasons — starters, bench, and IR — by player name.

Data through Week 17, 2024 · v1.3 · rebuilt July 20, 2026
